page: Amazon Web Services i4g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 64-bit architecture (Neoverse-N1) running at 2.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is bundled with 3750 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieved a multi-core stress-ng score of 24,287.8 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8,598.0. Memory read bandwidth for small cached blocks is high at 947.29 GB/sec, while large block read and write speeds reach 166.68 GB/sec and 74.4 GB/sec respectively. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core scoring 976.0 and single-threaded gzip compression rated as weak. Cryptographic performance is also weak, as shown by the Passmark CPU encryption score of 2,189.96.

This instance is designed for storage-intensive workloads that benefit from high-density local NVMe storage and ARM-based resource efficiency. It is highly suitable for database operations, scoring 10,352.38 on Passmark database benchmarks, and Redis deployments, which achieved 18.83 million SET operations per second. It is also capable of handling static web serving and multi-threaded decompression tasks. It is not recommended for heavy single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM inference due to poor performance in those specific benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services i4g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 3750 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it offers a 9 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8,598.0 and strong database operations at 10,352.38 on Passmark. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 976.0 and lower single-threaded compression speeds. This instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy workloads by combining high-capacity local NVMe storage with energy-efficient ARM architecture. It is best suited for database hosting, Redis key-value stores, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing, while being less optimal for single-threaded applications or large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3750 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, ideal for database and Redis workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance with 16 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3750 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM-based, storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSDs and balanced multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
Bundled 3750 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong database performance with 10,352.38 Passmark database score
Tradeoff of weaker single-core and cryptographic performance
Best suited for databases, Redis deployments, and static web serving
